rulings.Case Title: FORD MOTOR CREDIT COMPANY vs. GARRETT, RUDOLPH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption; Ntc Of Oppos. To Claim Of Exemption And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 30, 2012, Line 1. PLAINTIFF FORD MOTOR CREDIT COMPANY'S Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Defendant is to bring to the hearing evidence of the $133.00 monthly medical expense Defendant claims, that was not on Defendant's financial statement. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GIANCARLO MARANGHI VS. BENNY CHETCUTI JR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 30, 2012, Line 1. DEFENDANT MAGNATE FUND #2 LLC, JOHN SIMONSE, MAGNATE FUND #1 LLC, MAGNATE FUND #3 LLC, JWS CAPITAL MANAGEMENT, INCC., LHJS INVESTMENTS, 27TH STREET ASSOCIATES LLC, SOUTH VAN NESS ASSOCIATES LLC, 20 PARKRIDGE LLC, 55 WOODWARD LLC DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the second cause of action (Conversion) as to all defendants and as to the first, third, and fifth causes of action (Fraud, Financial Abuse of an Elder, and Declaratory Relief) as to defendants Magnate Fund #1 LLC, Magnate Fund #3 LLC, JWS Capital Management, LHJS Investments, 27th Street Associates LLC, South Van Ness Associates LLC, and 55 Woodward LLC, and overruled as to the fourth cause of action (Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress). As to the second cause of action, Plaintiff is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege terms of the loan which show he is entitled to immediate possession of the disputed funds. As to the first, third and fifth causes of action, Plaintiff is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege facts to show that defendants Magnate Fund #1 LLC, Magnate Fund #3 LLC, JWS Capital Management, LHJS Investments, 27th Street Associates LLC, South Van Ness Associates LLC, and 55 Woodward LLC were connected to the alleged fraud. The other claims are adequately pled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SHAWN-MICHAEL MATHIES VS. UNION BANK, N.A.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 30, 2012, Line 2. PLAINTIFF SHAWN-MICHAEL MATHIES AND KIM GIORDANO'S Re-Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. It would be very helpful to the court if counsel for Defendant Union Bank could be prepared to answer the following questions at the hearing: (1) How many emails are retrieved by searching for all emails sent from a Union Bank domain and addressed to plaintiff's Union Bank email account? (2) How many emails are retrieved by running keyword searches for all emails sent from a Union Bank' domain and addressed to plaintiff's Union Bank email account that contain any of the following keywords: "Overtime", "Hours", "Teller", "Daily Operation Guide", "Cold Call", "Customer Service", "Weekend"? Backup Media that is not readily accessible need not be searched. Each party is to email a proposed order in advance of the hearing to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a 'cc to opposing counsel. Each party should bring a hard-copy of its proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ANGELICA M. GONZALEZ VS. ALFONSO PICAZO, AS TRUSTEES OF THE ARTHUR AND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendants, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, APRIL 30, 2012, LINE 19, PLAINTIFF ANGELICA GONZALEZ Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendants, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants. Granted in part and denied in part. The request to compel responses is granted. Defendants shall serve responses to the discovery requests by May 7, 2012. The requests to establish admissions and for monetary sanctions are denied. The court reconciles the parties' positions by assuming that the discovery was properly served, but was not received and thus the appropriate solution is for the discovery to be responded to as soon as possible without evidentiary or monetary consequences suffered by the defendants. rulings.Case Title: KERN, INYO & MONO COUNTIES PLUMBING PLUMING et al VS. CALIFORNIA APPRENTICESHIP COUNCIL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Adminstrative Mandamus Or Mandate; Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 30, 2012, Line 9. PETITIONER KERN, INYO & MONO COUNTIES PLUMBING PLUMING, PIPEFITTER & REFRIGERATION/AIR CONDITIONING Petition For Writ Of Adminstrative Mandamus Or Mandate. Granted. The applicable mandamus statute is Code of Civil Procedure 1094.5. The Council's findings that petitioner neglected or refused to dispatch sufficient apprentices and petitioner does not have the capacity to dispatch sufficient apprentices are not supported by substantial evidence. The phrase "identified ¿ as deficient" in subsection 3075(b)(3) is a term of art that must be interpreted in light of and in conjunction with the phrase "identified as having deficiencies" in subsection 3073.1(c) relating to audits and thus any purported shortcoming of petitioner not previously identified in an audit per section 3073.1 is not a legal basis for a finding of deficiency for purposes of subsection 3075(b)(3). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, petitioner is required to prepare a proposed statement of decision in accordance with the tentative ruling. If the tentative ruling is contested, at the conclusion of the hearing the court will state which party or parties will be requested to prepare a proposed statement of decision. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL STEPHENS et al VS. FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY, A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 30, 2012, Line 11. DEFENDANT AMERICAN PACIFIC INSURANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. After review of the supplemental papers, the court re-affirms the earlier tentative ruling as follows: Sustained without leave to amend as to all five causes of action. The choice of law issue is an issue of law that is amenable to resolution on a demurrer. (Kearney v. Salomon Smith Barney, Inc. (2006) 39 Cal. 4th 95; Vaughn v. LJ International, Inc. (2009) 174 Cal. App. 4th 213; Cable v. Sahara Tahoe Corporation (1979) 93 Cal. App. 3d 384 (affirming Judge Robert Weil's sustaining of a demurrer without leave to amend on the basis of a choice of law ruling)). For purposes of choice of law, the relevant issue is which state law (California or Hawaii) applies to the obligation, if any, of APIC to pay the fees and costs of plaintiffs' retained independent counsel. (Hartford Underwriters Insurance Company v. Foundation Health Services, Inc. (5th Cir. 2008) 524 F.3d 588; Northern Insurance Company of New York v. Allied Mutual Insurance Company (9th Cir.1992) 955 F.2d 1353, 1359-60). This issue is to be resolved by using California's governmental interest analysis. (Kearney). The law of California as set forth in Civil Code 2860 and the Cumis decision materially differs from the law of Hawaii as set forth in Finley v. The Home Insurance Company (HI 1998) 975 P.2d 1145. A true conflict exists because there is a material difference in the interests of the two states in their differing rules. California's rule of requiring an insurer to pay for independent counsel retained by the insured once there is a reservation of rights is based on California's interest in protecting an insured when there is a conflict of interest between the insured and the insurer. (Cumis; Northern Insurance Company). While Hawaii's contrary rule is based on Hawaii's interest in protecting an insured when there is a conflict of interest between the insured and the insurer, the rule is also based on Hawaii's interest in preserving the right of an insurer to select counsel for whom it is paying the counsel's fees. (Finley). Here, where the underlying lawsuit was venued in Hawaii and the insurance policy was issued by a Hawaiian insurer to insure a Hawaii residence of California residents on insurance forms intended to be used for Hawaiian insurance policies, the interests of Hawaii would be much more impaired if California law applied than the interests of California would be impaired if Hawaii law applied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, APRIL 30, 2012, LINE 17, DEFENDANT DIANE FAIRCHILD Motion To Quash Service Of Summons. Granted. No opposition was filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted with the moving papers. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES HADEN VS. INTERCONTINENTAL MARK HOPKINS SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, APRIL 30, 2012, LINE 18, DEFENDANT PETER KOEHLER, NELUM GUNEWARDANE, INTER-CONTINENTAL HOTELS CORPORATION MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Granted. Defendant correctly cites and applies Gardner and Ricketts, which under the facts alleged limits plaintiffs' recovery to $500 ($250 for each earring). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)
